text: What strategies did you use to solve this problem that involve ratios and unit rates?

Students will continue to build expertise in working with unit rates to solve ratio problems of greater complexity.

Students mat mention a variety of strategies, including

  • unit rates;
  • ratio tables;
  • multiplying/dividing both quantities in a ratio (scaling) to find larger or smaller values as needed; and
  • algebraic expression to generate equivalent ratios.

Did You Know?

Making and playing with slime is a favorite activity among children. One type of slime in our own bodies is saliva, and our saliva is different from that of other species.

Our bodies contain a form of slimy mucus that keeps our mouths wet. Mucus is a form of a gel that has different tasks. In humans, it helps our immune systems, lubricates our esophagus so food can move easily, protects our stomach from acids, and makes snot. The mucus contains proteins called mucins.

Mucins occur in many species. For example, mucins create pearls in mollusks. Researchers are interested in how these mucins evolved and how they are related across species. "The researchers theorized there is a reason for so many orphan mucin genes, particularly in saliva, across species: It is easier for them to evolve."

Source: Johnson, E., "The Secret Success of Slime," U.S. National Science Foundation, October 25, 2022.

Accessed online.
